figures.caption: Figure 2: Sweep-field analysis. Top: four operating regimes; the velocity-failure boundary (red band) is sharp, confirming Theorem 7.3. Bottom: theory–empirical gap at small ε reflects summarisation overhead, which does not affect the scheduling gain.

Figure 1: PSMAS overview. (a) Agents on S1 by topological phase; sweep φ(t) (red) activates only A3 (within ε). (b) Dependency DAG with TPA phase labels derived by eq. (2). (c) Active agents receive full context; idle agents receive a compressed summary of length αL. Scheduling and compression are decoupled (§6): scheduling controls the idle fraction; compression controls the residual idle cost.
